title: Kochi Corporation receives nine designs for proposed dog shelters at Brahmapuram
content: In the first phase, the civic body plans to build shelters with a capacity to accommodate around 1,000 strays on one acre of land, adjacent to the Corporation’s Animal Birth Control Centre at Brahmapuram, in compliance with the recent Supreme Court verdict

title: Amit Shah directs NAFED, NCCF to bypass middlemen, buy pulses and oilseeds directly from farmers
content: Amit Shah set a two-year deadline, saying farmers would be able to sell directly to the two cooperatives and receive payment without intermediaries

title: BJP objects to Minister Nirmalkumar’s Governor appointment remark; demands apology
content: Mr. Nirmalkumar, during a press interaction recently, had said that even relatives of PM Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah could be appointed to the post of Governor

title: Nandyal Collector sets July 1 deadline to finish 2,103 Jaladhara water works
content: Of 2,103 works sanctioned, 1,587 are done and 516 are pending; a Central Water Commission team will verify the structures built under NREGS

title: Japanese PM Sanai Takaichi’s Guwahati visit cancelled, meetings will be hosted in Delhi
content: Earlier in 2019, the late Japanese PM Shinzo Abe too was expected to visit Assam for a bilateral meeting with PM Modi when the event had to be cancelled because of protests related to the Citizenship Amendment Act

title: Kodagu villagers’ opposition forces spiritual guru Sri M to withdraw proposed meditation retreat
content: Villagers cite ecological, cultural and religious concerns as spiritual guru cancels planned development near a hill after sustained protests

title: Vijayanagara king’s 16th-century inscriptions come to light in Seshachalam forest, Tirupati
content: One text links the find to Gudimallam, where the King granted temple lands to fund worship at a shrine long overshadowed by its neighbours

title: KSIIDC hands over ₹143 crore dividend to government of Karnataka
content: M.B. Patil, Minister for Large and Medium Industries, and Infrastructure Development, said that KSIIDC had recorded a net profit of ₹188.41 crore during 2024-25 after tax

title: Kozhikode’s forest cover holds steady at 1,882 sq km
content: With forests covering 80.26% of its land area, Kozhikode ranks first in Kerala in forest cover proportion

title: Ayodhya Ram temple donation case: SIT submits preliminary report to U.P. government
content: The Uttar Pradesh government had on June 13 constituted the SIT on a request from the temple trust, following allegations of misappropriation of donations received at the Ayodhya Ram temple

title: CAG flags ‘variation’ of ₹12,669 crore in Kerala’s KIIFB, KSSPL off-Budget borrowings for 2024-25
content: Reiterating its concerns regarding off-Budget borrowings made through these agencies, the CAG has recommended that the Kerala government disclose the details of such borrowings in the Budget as well as to the Union government

title: Supreme Court stays premature release of Bowbazar blast convict
content: The actions of Rashid Khan amounted to ‘almost a terrorist activity’ and he had played the ‘role of a mastermind’, the Bench orally remarks

title: Every file represents aspirations, lives of countless citizens: PM Modi to young IAS officers
content: Prime Minister Modi told the young civil servants to dedicate themselves to nation-building with a strong sense of purpose, innovation and citizen-centric governance, and urged them to always remember the human impact behind every administrative file.

title: AAP MLA Vasava, 8 others get 7 years in jail for assaulting forest officials, extortion
content: Additional Sessions Judge A.V. Hirpara convicted Vasava, his wife Shakuntala Vasava and others in connection with the incident that had occurred on the night of October 30, 2023

title: Over 6 lakh vehicles hit Hyderabad’s IT corridor as overnight rain leaves roads waterlogged, snarls rush-hour traffic
content: Nearly a million vehicles were recorded in Cyberabad Police Commissionerate limits by noon on June 23, 2026, even as the police asked companies to consider skeleton staffing and remote working arrangements

title: 1,300 units of blood collected at camp organised by JK Tyre in Mysuru
content: The camp is organised to mark the birth anniversary of former chairperson (late) Hari Shankar Singhania

title: Duvvada Srinivas denies remarks against Pawan Kalyan, says video was AI-made to defame him
content: He denies any derogatory remarks against the leaders and alleges the Minister targeted Kalinga and Kapu community figures in the Opposition

title: FCRA Rules tightened: NGOs must declare social media accounts, stick to specified activities; political content barred
content: All NGOs and associations registered under FCRA, 2010 are required to disclose specific activities and geographical scope of their programmes

title: Three militants arrested in Manipur
content: The insurgents, belonging to two different banned outfits, were nabbed from Imphal West and Thoubal districts

title: Devastating building fire in Lucknow brings development authority’s actions under scrutiny
content: The building had been issued a demolition order in 2016 over unauthorised construction, but it was revoked within two months

title: IT Ministry asks CJP’s Dipke to be present for hearing on X account ban
content: The union government asked Abhijeet Dipke to be present for an inter-ministerial committee hearing on June 24, 2026 to hear his appeal against the blocking of the Cockroach Janta Party’s initial X handle

title: PU lecturers to protest against amended recruitment rules in Karnataka
content: They pointed out that PU lecturers are recruited to the gazetted Group-B academic cadre, and that deputing them to high schools would undermine the existing cadre structure

title: El Nino fear may cut Vizianagaram crop area by 20,000 hectares this Kharif
content: Paddy may fall to 1.03 lakh hectares from 1.21 lakh; officials are urging farmers to shift to crops that need less water

title: Lucknow building fire: FIR cites illegal commercial use, single entry/exit in building that claimed 15 lives
content: The FIR stated that the fire broke out around 2:30 p.m. on June 22, at the pet shop and clinic, following which firefighters, SDRF, NDRF and police personnel carried out rescue operations

title: Hyderabad Metro Rail take over and expansion talks in Delhi yield positive outcome; SBICAPS to act as consultant
content: CM A. Revanth Reddy convinces Union Ministers Ashwini Vaishnaw and Manoharlal Khattar about the Telangana government’s decision to take over the project and propose its expansion

title: Karnataka: Buoyed by beer, Excise revenue grows by 14.84% in a month following reforms
content: The sale of beer between May 11 and June 11 grew by 54.67%; the sale of Indian Made Liquor in the same period came down marginally by 0.35%

title: Behind ‘Son of Thanjai’, the Tamil game that promises to revolutionise Indian gaming
content: Writer-lyricist Madhan Karky and Ayelet Studio head Abraham K take us through the making of their historical open-world game and speak about the challenges of making a rooted Tamil game with international appeal

title: Gade Sai Krishna case: SIT picks up suspended CI from hideout in Vijayawada
content: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) police shifted him to the Task Force office for questioning in the alleged custodial torture, death and screening of evidence in the case

title: Follow protocol at government events, Speaker tells officials
content: He instructs the Chief Secretary to issue instructions to Municipal Commissioners, Commissioners of Municipal Corporations, officials of local bodies and Collectors to adhere to the warrant of precedence

title: Pulse Polio drive on June 28 to cover 1.89 lakh children in Visakhapatnam
content: Children will get drops at 1,062 booths on June 28, and teams will make door-to-door visits on June 29 and 30 to reach those missed

title: NSA Doval meets counterparts from BRICS grouping, discusses West Asia and India-China ties
content: MEA says Doval and Iran’s Nezamipour reviewed the situation in West Asia and discussed cooperation under the BRICS platform and India-Iran bilateral ties. On meeting with China’s Wang, it says the two sides noted progress towards gradual normalisation of ties

title: Triumph of medicos in Telangana polls sparks calls for doctor-led Health Department
content: A doctor, appointed as Minister for Health, will have clearer insights into patient-care, hospital management and the intricacies of medical profession, say associations after 15 doctors were elected MLAs in State

title: CAG criticises Kerala govt for ‘irregular’ withdrawals from treasury savings bank accounts
content: CAG report on State Finances for 2024-25 tabled in Kerala Assembly describes certain resumptions of funds as ‘serious breach of accountability and financial propriety’

title: No ‘double standards’ in fight against terrorism, says NSA Ajit Doval in Russia
content: The National Security Adviser participated in the first International Security Forum and the 14th Meeting of High Representatives for Security Matters in Moscow

title: Monsoon advances in Maharashtra; IMD forecasts rain until June 26
content: Madhya Maharashtra will see thunderstorms with lightning, winds, and moderate to heavy rainfall during the same period

title: North-eastern flourish at the ballot
content: Assam’s 85 per cent turnout this time is in keeping with the traditional high voting figures in the State and its neighbours

title: Operation Toofan: Law will apply equally to film personalities and VIPs in drug cases, says Ramesh Chennithala
content: Referring to alleged prevalence of drug use at DJ parties, luxury hotels, and film-related gatherings, Home Minister says there will be strong action

title: Pune businessman’s son pushed into gorge: How fiancée allegedly tried to pass it off as accident
content: A trek to Lohagad Fort, initially thought to be a tragic accident, has been revealed as a murder. Police arrested a woman and her alleged lover for allegedly pushing her fiancé into a deep gorge. The victim, Ketan Vishal Agrawal, was lured to the fort under the guise of birthday celebrations. Investigators uncovered a conspiracy driven by the woman's alleged unwillingness to marry and her relationship with another man.

title: Why did stock market crash today? Sensex plunges 900 points - top reasons for fall
content: Around Rs 4.61 lakh crore in investor wealth was wiped as the combined market capitalisation of BSE-listed companies dropped to about Rs 475 lakh crore. BSE Sensex ended around 900 points down, dropped to levels just above 76,200. Nifty50 also dropped below 23,850.

title: IPL trade: Pant returns to DC with Rs 12 crore cut, Kuldeep joins LSG
content: The IPL confirmed the move on Tuesday, with Pant agreeing to a salary of Rs 15 crore, Rs 12 crore less than the record Rs 27 crore deal he received when he joined Lucknow Super Giants ahead of the 2025 season.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership
content: Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions.  These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13% drop in its workforce.  The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers.  This move comes amidst broader concerns about AI's impact on tech jobs.

title: Trump waives sanctions on Iran crude oil: What it means for India
content: As part of a broader package designed to provide economic relief to Iran, the US Treasury announced a sanctions waiver valid until August 21. The exemption allows Tehran to export oil and related products and receive payments for those sales.

title: Gavaskar pushes for rest window, says player rotation is 'cheapening the India cap'
content: His comments come after India completed a 3-0 ODI series sweep over Afghanistan, shortly after the conclusion of the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 season.

title: Chandigarh homes get costlier: How new collector rates affect property registration
content: As of April 1, homebuyers in Chandigarh will encounter elevated costs linked to new collector rates for properties. These adjustments, notably affecting prime real estate, result in increased stamp duty and registration fees. Even if the purchase price remains unchanged, buyers must anticipate higher registration costs stemming from the government's revised minimum property valuations.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: US-Iran MoU outlines $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund — but who will pay the bill?
content: US secretary of state Marco Rubio is heading to the Gulf amid controversy over a proposed $300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran. While critics decry it as a US payout, the administration claims Gulf states and private investors would largely finance it. However, some Arab allies fear a wealthier Iran could boost its regional influence and military might, sparking unease despite suggestions of significant foreign interest in investing.

title: What is “quiet cracking”? Why more parents are struggling in silence
content: Parents are increasingly experiencing 'quiet cracking,' a silent burnout where they maintain outward functionality while feeling overwhelmed internally. This phenomenon stems from immense pressure from work, home, and emotional labor, exacerbated by societal expectations and a lack of support. The article highlights the need for reduced expectations, genuine connection, and practical backup to help parents cope and prevent this widespread exhaustion.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: 'Engine of loot': Congress alleges MP CM Mohan Yadav involved in corruption, cites media report
content: "Mohan Yadav, has become the mastermind of this loot. There is also talk that the "farming of news" against the chief minister has been orchestrated by the agriculture minister who went from MP to the center! It seems like infighting over the chair and shares in the loot," Congress leader Jairam Ramesh said in a post on X.

title: CUET UG result 2026 declared, download score cards here
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has declared the CUET UG Result 2026, enabling candidates to download their score cards from the official website. Conducted for over 15.68 lakh registered candidates, the examination will determine undergraduate admissions for the 2026-27 academic session across participating universities. The NTA has also released key examination statistics, performance highlights and topper details. Candidates should now download their score cards and complete university-specific admission and counselling formalities.

title: Tom Brady sends clear message after Gisele’s alleged Father’s Day snub
content: Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bundchen, has continued to dominate headlines even after years of their divorce. The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bundchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are.

title: New Champions crowned, shocking betrayal headlines explosive WWE Raw Night
content: London's O2 Arena witnessed a dramatic WWE RAW go-home show for Night of Champions.  A beloved star turned heel, shocking fans, while Chad Gable faced his former stablemates.  Key bouts saw LA Knight triumph over Jimmy Uso amidst Bloodline interference, and The Street Profits capture the WWE World Tag Team Titles in a chaotic main event, culminating in a surprise championship coronation.

title: Who is Geno Smith’s alleged Ex? Inside the claims stirring NFL attention
content: The New York Jets’ star player, Geno Smith, was gearing up for the next season when he found himself in major legal trouble after an alleged ex girlfriend accused him of domestic violence and being neglectful of a child just a few hours ago. The controversial accusations have sparked shockwaves across the league and many are curious about Geno Smith’s alleged ex girlfriend.

title: Red alert for rain after dust storms, strong winds hit Delhi-NCR
content: Delhi-NCR experienced dust storms and strong winds Tuesday afternoon, prompting the India Meteorological Department to issue a red alert. Severe thunderstorms, lightning, and squally winds up to 100 kmph are anticipated across most of the capital in the coming hours. Light to moderate rainfall is also expected in isolated areas. Authorities have been alerted to take necessary precautions.

title: UAE eyes India’s BrahMos, Akashteer: How strike-and-shield systems could boost Gulf defence
content: The UAE is reportedly in talks with India to acquire BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles and the Akashteer air-defence system. This move signifies Abu Dhabi's strategy to bolster both offensive and defensive capabilities while diversifying its military suppliers beyond traditional Western partners. The potential deal aims to enhance precision strike abilities and improve aerial threat response, reflecting the UAE's response to evolving regional security challenges.

title: Suryansh Shedge replaces Nitish Reddy in India squads for Ireland & England T20Is
content: Suryansh Shedge has been called up to the Indian squad for the upcoming T20I series against Ireland and England, replacing the injured Nitish Kumar Reddy. Reddy sustained a quadriceps injury and will undergo rehabilitation. Shedge earned his maiden national call-up following stellar performances for India A in Sri Lanka and for Mumbai in the Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy, showcasing his potent finishing abilities.

title: 'Papa, please save me': Man stood helpless as fire trapped son inside Lucknow coaching centre
content: A devastating fire in Aliganj claimed several young lives, leaving families shattered. Among the victims were Abdul Rahman, the sole breadwinner for his paralyzed father and homemaker mother; Sukhmani Singh, who made a desperate plea for help in a final call; Mohammad Shahzan, a beloved only son; and Aditya Srivastava, who pleaded for rescue before succumbing to the blaze. The tragedy has plunged multiple households into profound grief.

title: Massive showdown in TN assembly: CM Vijay targets DMK, mimics Stalin; opposition walks out
content: Tamil Nadu chief minister Vijay engaged in a heated exchange with the opposition DMK in the assembly, accusing them of misusing party funds and engaging in political opportunism. Amidst disruptions, he mimicked a viral hand gesture associated with former CM MK Stalin, leading to a DMK walkout. Vijay defended his party's alliances and addressed concerns on law and order, asserting his government's commitment to tackling corruption and crime.

title: 'Starstruck' by Sooryavanshi: Broad quips 'he's even getting into sledging now'
content: Former England pacer Stuart Broad expressed immense excitement to witness 15-year-old Indian batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi during the upcoming UK T20I series, admitting he might be starstruck. Sooryavanshi's record-breaking List-A fifty has propelled him into India's senior squad, drawing praise from Broad and Jos Buttler for his explosive talent and rapid rise, making him a significant challenge for England.

title: Why Indians aren't giving up on SIPs despite muted market returns
content: Despite a sluggish stock market and foreign investor sell-offs, Indian retail investors are consistently investing through SIPs, showing remarkable resilience.  Monthly SIP inflows surged significantly, becoming the primary demand driver for domestic equities.  This sustained participation, fueled by favorable policies and a 'set-and-forget' approach, highlights a strong underlying investor confidence in the market's long-term prospects.

title: Message seen on WhatsApp: Kunal Shah gets real CRED from Meta
content: Kunal Shah is an interesting choice, because WhatsApp does not need a leader to teach people how to send a message; it needs one who understands how trust, status, habit, and small transactions mutate inside closed networks, which is, almost exactly, the discipline CRED was built to practise.

title: Murder, not fall: Pune businessman's son killed at Lohagad Fort; fiancée, friend detained
content: A Pune trekker's death at Lohagad fort, initially ruled an accident, has taken a shocking turn. Police now suspect his fiancée and her male friend deliberately pushed him into a deep gorge. The couple has been detained as the investigation shifts to a murder probe, casting a dark shadow over the victim's upcoming November wedding.

title: El Nino woes: Why weak monsoon may turn out to be a bigger worry for India than US-Iran war
content: A normal monsoon is important for several reasons - most of all for the way it feeds into the economy’s growth and inflationary routes. A below average monsoon impacts crop sowing and harvest, which in turn leads to prices of vegetables and staple food rising.

title: Beyond defence corridors: MoD eyes 7 clusters to boost India’s military manufacturing
content: India is launching seven specialized manufacturing clusters to bolster its defence industry, a nationwide initiative distinct from existing corridors. This framework aims to unite states, industry, and academia, focusing on areas like exports, skill development, and innovation. States are assigned specific roles, fostering a self-reliant and globally competitive defence sector aligned with Aatmanirbhar Bharat.

title: 11-year-old Delhi girl kidnapped, raped and murdered near Gurgaon border; 1 held
content: A horrific incident has gripped Delhi as an 11-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped, brutally assaulted, and murdered after being taken from a South Delhi footpath. Police swiftly arrested a suspect following a rigorous investigation involving CCTV analysis and technical evidence. The victim's body was discovered in a forested area near the Delhi-Gurugram border. Authorities are continuing their interrogation to uncover any potential accomplices.

title: Amid rebellion, TMC sends fresh office-bearers list to EC, says Mamata remains party chief
content: Trinamool Congress has reshuffled its national working committee, asserting Mamata Banerjee's leadership following a significant electoral setback. This move aims to counter a rebellion by a faction claiming to be the "real" TMC, which appointed its own chairperson. The party has informed the Election Commission of its new office-bearers, with Banerjee remaining chairperson and her nephew Abhishek Banerjee continuing as national general secretary.

title: 'France will probably beat us': Haaland's reality check ahead of Mbappe showdown
content: The Manchester City forward now has four goals in the World Cup, level with France's Kylian Mbappe and one behind Lionel Messi. Norway's victory also takes them to six points in Group I, alongside France, with the two sides set to meet on Friday to decide the group winner.

title: Bhu-Aadhaar for land: How a 14-digit ID can help property buyers to avoid disputes
content: India is introducing Bhu-Aadhaar, a 14-digit Unique Land Parcel Identification Number (ULPIN), to digitally identify every land parcel. This initiative aims to simplify property verification by creating a standard digital record, making transactions easier for buyers, banks, and government agencies. Based on geo-referenced coordinates, ULPIN ensures a unique digital identity tied to each plot's exact location, strengthening land administration and potentially reducing disputes.

title: 'India welcomes US-Iran MoU': NSA Ajit Doval expresses 'cautious optimism' over peace deal
content: India's National Security Advisor Ajit Doval expressed 'cautious optimism' regarding the US-Iran MoU, hoping it restores regional stability and strengthens energy security. He highlighted the potential for easing global supply chain disruptions and improving economic prosperity through the Strait of Hormuz. Doval also addressed the world's complex security landscape and the weakening of multilateral institutions, emphasizing BRICS' role in fostering a multipolar order and cooperation among emerging economies.

title: Ayodhya donation scam probe: SIT submits preliminary report to UP govt
content: A Special Investigation Team has submitted its initial findings to the Uttar Pradesh government regarding allegations of embezzlement of donations for the Ayodhya Ram temple. The probe, initiated by the temple trust, is actively continuing as investigators gather more information. Officials confirmed the preliminary report was handed over to the Additional Chief Secretary (Home) by the Lucknow Divisional Commissioner, with further details expected as the investigation progresses.

title: Quote of the Day by Mammootty
content: Veteran actor Mammootty, known for his grounded perspective, shared profound thoughts on fame and remembrance in a recent interview. He candidly stated, "Don’t expect people to remember you until the end of the world," emphasizing the transient nature of public memory. Mammootty, who has no plans for retirement, believes that even great individuals are remembered for a limited time, urging a focus on present value rather than eternal recognition.

title: 'Felt like a dream': Sooryavanshi gets emotional after getting India jersey - Watch
content: At 15 years and 71 days, Sooryavanshi has become the youngest player selected in India's senior men's squad, surpassing the record set by Sachin Tendulkar 36 years ago.

title: Cocktail 2 Box Office Collection Day 5: Can Shahid Kapoor's film cross Rs 60 crore?
content: Homi Adajania's 'Cocktail 2', starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon, and Rashmika Mandanna, registered a steep 62% drop on its first Monday, collecting Rs 6.75 crore net after a stellar Rs 47.50 crore three-day opening weekend. The film has headed into Day 5 today since its release on June 19, 2026. With a reported production budget of Rs 150 crore, the film's weekday hold has become critical to reaching break-even and the clock is ticking, with Akshay Kumar's 'Welcome to the Jungle' set to arrive in cinemas this Friday alongside a resurgent 'Main Vaapas Aaunga.

title: 'Bring a diaper, write your demand': CJP's new protest plan at 6pm today
content: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continues its four-day protest at Jantar Mantar, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG paper leak. Organizers reported police attempts to restrict protest space overnight. The CJP has announced a "diaper donation drive" and dedicated a day for UPSC and SSC aspirants to voice concerns, vowing to continue their indefinite agitation.

title: ITR filing FY 2025-26: How to calculate taxes under old income tax regime - explained
content: For many people the old income tax regime continues to be attractive despite the new regime offering higher basic exemption and standard deduction, rebate limits and lower tax rates.

title: Will Ronaldo be benched vs Uzbekistan? Portugal coach drops hints before match day
content: Portugal's coach Roberto Martínez remained tight-lipped about Cristiano Ronaldo's starting position for the upcoming Uzbekistan match, following a subdued performance against Congo. Despite external criticism, Martínez defended his squad, emphasising unity and dismissing 'unfair' commentary. He highlighted Ronaldo's continued value in creating space and scoring opportunities.

title: 'Called to shoot reels, then raped': Delhi influencer alleges sexual assault in Burari hotel
content: A social media influencer has accused a man of rape, alleging he lured her to a hotel for video shoots and then sexually assaulted her. The incident, which occurred in September 2022, was followed by alleged blackmail and repeated assaults. The victim also reported threats from the accused's relatives after she ended contact. Police have registered an FIR and are investigating.

title: Collector seeks five-year data as SC Commission readies Visakhapatnam review on June 27
content: The Collector has sought records on the SC/ST Atrocities Act, civil rights enforcement and land issues, ahead of the Commission’s June 27 review

title: Bengaluru’s Peenya flyover declared safe after successful load test
content: The flyover, one of Bengaluru’s busiest stretches, has undergone repeated repair and maintenance works since 2021, particularly involving the replacement and strengthening of cables.

title: Parambikulam-Aliyar Project: CAG pulls up Kerala Forest dept for failing to revise licence fee on land leased to Tamil Nadu
content: Non-levy of revised licence fee from Tamil Nadu led to a shortfall in revenue  amounting to ₹1.14 crore for the period from 2012-13 to 2023-24, calculated for an extent of 2,455.23 hectares, along with an interest liability of ₹56 lakh calculated at annual interest rate of 12%, according to CAG report

title: CJP protest in Delhi: Dipke, supporters continue sit-in overnight at Jantar Mantar; urge people to join protest
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who joined the protest on June 20, announced that he would go on a hunger strike on June 27 if Union Education Miniser Dharmendra Pradhan did not step down

title: CJP protesters remain at Jantar Mantar for third consecutive day
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke announced that the protest would continue on Monday, June 22, and appealed to farmer unions and members of the public to join the movement.

title: Operation Toofan: 36 kg of cannabis seized, two held in Thrissur
content: Kunnamkulam police and DANSAF team intercept accused after dramatic escape bid, officers have a narrow escape as accused try to ram their vehicle into police vehicle

title: Lucknow building fire: Mother's plea, colleague’s distress call capture horror
content: The blaze erupted at the building housing an animation centre on Usha Mehta Marg, leaving 15 people dead and seven injured

title: Watch: U.K. PM Keir Starmer resigns: What went wrong?
content: In this episode, we look at the resignation of U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer amid mounting political pressure, the deadly ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing unit in Tamil Nadu’s Tiruvallur district, and  more.

title: Ongole GGH treats rare Guillain-Barré case free, sparing a bill of up to ₹6 lakh
content: Nageswar Reddy, a 29-year-old technician who lost the use of his hands, was given IVIG over five days and has recovered

title: How everyday Kerala is being reimagined in an award-nominated illustration series
content: lllustrator Mohammed Sajid’s portrait series is in contention at the AOI World Illustration Awards 2026, spotlighting fish sellers, flower vendors, postmen and other familiar faces of Kerala’s streets

title: Wang Yi calls for India, China to accelerate resumption of stalled dialogue mechanisms
content: A lengthier Chinese readout of the talks between NSA Ajit Doval and China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi said that the Chinese leader noted that “India is an important neighbour of China”

title: West Bengal government rolls out benefits to 28 lakh women under Annapurna Yojana
content: West Bengal Chief Secretary Manoj Agarwal said that the beneficiaries of Annapurna Yojana will be made public and people will have opportunity to file objections in any non deserving beneficiary is receiving benefit.

title: 'Access to Drinking Water A Right': Bombay High Court Flags Scarcity In Maharashtra
content: The Bombay High Court has held that access to clean and potable drinking water is a fundamental right and questioned why citizens in Maharashtra continue to approach courts for such a basic necessity even after 75 years of Independence.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Pakistan Is Becoming Harder To Isolate - Despite India's Push
content: Pakistan's willingness to sell itself to the highest bidder explains its curious positioning in the world. India's only option now is to get creative as it tries to isolate it globally.

title: Bharat Tiwari Case: JDU, BJP Assure Fair Probe, Say No Injustice Will Be Done
content: Bihar's ruling Janata Dal-United (JD-U) and the BJP on Tuesday asserted that no injustice would be done to anyone in the Bharat Tiwari encounter case and reaffirmed their commitment to ensuring a fair and transparent investigation under the state's 'sushasan' (good governance) framework.

title: Akasa Air Eyes 30% Growth, Says Long-Term Expansion Unaffected By Global Trends
content: In a conversation with NDTV, Goel said the airline's long-term expansion strategy remains unchanged even as the aviation sector grapples with rising operational costs amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Drops 893 Points Amid Weak Global Cues, Crude Over $78
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Brent crude gained 0.38% to $78.15 a barrel and US WTI rose to $74.19 a barrel.

title: Apple, Tesla Secrets Leaked In Tata Electronics Cyberbreach? What We Know
content: Researchers who examined the leaked database found folders and documents that appear to be connected to Apple and Tesla.

title: 'Wife Looking For Husband': Udhayanidhi Stalin's Personal Attack On Vijay
content: U Stalin, who is the opposition leader in the Tamil Nadu Assembly, mentioned that the state knows the "story of the wife looking for a husband in the Chengalpattu court", a reference to Vijay's wife Sangeetha Sornalingam filing the divorce petition in the Chengalpattu court.

title: "B Team Of Disruptive Elements": Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan To NDTV On CJP
content: Those who were rejected in the democracy have come in disguise and are now after the system, said Dharmendra Pradhan, slamming the CJP.

title: Kolkata Woman Recalls Last Call With Daughter Killed In Lucknow Fire
content: The family, comprising her father Vishwanath, mother, brother Akash and uncle Palash Kar, rushed to Lucknow after being informed about the tragedy by Kolkata police.

title: From Drones To New Battle Formations: The Legacy Of General Upendra Dwivedi
content: General Dwivedi pursued capability-centric warfare as a successor to the platform-centric models that had long defined Indian military thinking.

title: 15-Year-Old Among 3 Dead After Truck Crashes Into Kerala Bus Stop
content: Five others sustained injuries in the accident, of whom three are reported to be in critical condition.
